name: claude-mnemonic
description: Two-stage retrieval memory system with bi-encoder semantic search and cross-encoder reranking, built on SQLite.
tagline: Observation-based memory with decay and lifecycle
approachDetail: Two-stage retrieval: bi-encoder semantic search then cross-encoder reranking. SQLite with FTS5 + sqlite-vec, ONNX embeddings
searchDetail: FTS5 for exact matches, sqlite-vec for semantic search, BGE cross-encoder reranking
storageDetail: SQLite with FTS5 and sqlite-vec, all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embeddings via ONNX
mcpDetail: Exposes search, timeline, decisions, changes, and how_it_works tools
scopesDetail: Auto-classifies as project-local or global based on tags like best-practice, security, architecture
duplicateDetail: Detects contradictory observations, memories can be superseded
retentionDetail: Observation lifecycle with supersede/archive, importance scoring with time decay and voting
crossMachineDetail: Local plugin, memories stay on one machine
webUiDetail: Dashboard at localhost:37777 for browsing and managing memories
tokenBudgetingDetail: Max 100 observations, 25 full-detail, 10 sessions per query — configurable limits
contextInjectionDetail: Agent calls MCP tools (search, timeline, decisions, changes) to retrieve context
auditTrailDetail: Memories can be superseded creating chains; importance scores decay over time
